senses.definition: the state or condition of having no fixed or identifiable roots or origins
examples: The immigrants felt a sense of rootlessness after moving to a new country.
Her rootlessness made it hard to settle in any one place.

senses.definition: a small root or a tiny part of a root
examples: The plant started to develop a new rootlet after the rain.
Each rootlet grows to help the plant absorb water.

senses.definition: multiple small roots or root-like structures
examples: The soil contained many rootlets from nearby plants.
Rootlets protected the plant by stabilizing it in loose soil.

senses.definition: having a shape or quality like a root; resembling a root
examples: The rootlike structures helped the plant cling to the soil.
They described the fungus as rootlike in appearance.

senses.definition: a small root or a playful or juvenile growth of a root
examples: A rootling appeared at the cut stem where the plant was damaged.
Gardeners sometimes gently tease out rootlings to transplant.

senses.definition: the parts of a plant that grow underground and take in water and nutrients
examples: Water the plant so its roots stay healthy.
Roots keep the tree firmly in the ground.

senses.definition: the origin or source of something
examples: The roots of the tradition lie in ancient rituals.
Understanding the roots of the problem helps resolve it.

senses.definition: a computer server that stores the root zone data in the Domain Name System (DNS)
examples: The rootserver responds to queries about top-level domains.
DNS queries may traverse several rootservers before reaching an authoritative server.

senses.definition: multiple root servers in the DNS system
examples: The rootservers form the highest level of the DNS hierarchy.
Changes to rootservers can affect many domains globally.

senses.definition: more rooted or more like a root in quality or appearance
examples: The rootsier soil held water better during the drought.
Her rootsier approach made the design feel grounded.

senses.definition: most rooted or most like a root in quality or appearance
examples: This is the rootsiest part of the garden, with dense, fibrous soil.
Among the samples, that one looked the rootsiest.

senses.definition: the quality of being rooted or having strong roots
examples: The plant's rootsiness helps it survive windy days.
Root depth contributes to the rootsiness of a shrub.

senses.definition: plural form of rootsiness; qualities of being rooted
examples: The field displayed varying degrees of rootsinesses among plants.
We observed several rootsinesses across the grove.

senses.definition: a stalk connecting a plant’s root system to its above-ground parts
examples: The rootstalk supported the young plant as it grew taller.
Cutting the rootstalk can prevent new growth.

senses.definition: multiple rootstalks
examples: The plant has several rootstalks that spread outward.
Damaged rootstalks can affect nutrient flow.

senses.definition: the part of a plant onto which another plant is grafted; the root system of a plant
examples: Grapes are often grown on a hardy rootstock.
The rootstock provides the plant with nutrients.

senses.definition: multiple rootstock plants or root systems used for grafting
examples: Different rootstocks were tested for disease resistance.
Nurseries stock several rootstocks for apple trees.

senses.definition: having roots or a root-like appearance; earthy or rustic in style
examples: The decor had a rootsy feel with natural wood and stone.
Her rootsy attitude matched the rural landscape.

senses.definition: a dance term or a coined word referring to a movement related to rooted or grounding steps (rare coined usage)
examples: The performance included a rootwalt inspired by grounded rhythms.

senses.definition: toward the root or toward the base of a plant
examples: Place the plant so its stem leans rootward for stability.
Coco coir helps direct moisture rootward.

senses.definition: in the direction of the roots; from the root outward
examples: The irrigation hose should run rootwise to hydrate the root zone.
Examine the plant rootwise to check for damage.

senses.definition: an insect larva that feeds on roots, harming plants
examples: The corn field was damaged by rootworms.
Farmers use crop rotation to reduce rootworm damage.

senses.definition: multiple rootworm insects or larvae
examples: Rootworms can devastate root systems if not managed.
Integrated pest management targets rootworms among other pests.

senses.definition: having roots; earthy; connected with roots
examples: The wine had a rooty aroma from the forest floor.
They preferred a rooty, rustic flavor in the soup.

senses.definition: a groove or furrow; a channel cut into a surface
examples: The wooden board has a shallow roove along its edge.
A small roove guides the slide in the mechanism.

senses.definition: having grooves or channels
examples: A rooved edge helps the panel fit into place.
The rooved texture creates grip.
